title: StatsD Metric Instance
description: The parsed, in-memory representation of a single StatsD metric observation after the daemon has parsed a wire-protocol line. Useful for tooling that bridges StatsD into JSON-native sinks (InfluxDB, OpenTelemetry, Kafka).

bucket.description: Dotted-path metric name. Conventional separator is `.`; clients commonly prefix with environment, service, and host.

value.description: Numeric observation. Signed for delta-style gauges (`+`, `-`). Sets accept arbitrary strings as values — see `setValue`.

setValue.description: String observation for set-type metrics; tracked for cardinality, not magnitude.

type.description: Metric type as decoded from the wire-format suffix (`c`, `g`, `ms`, `h`, `s`, `m`, `d`).

delta.description: True when a gauge value carried a `+` or `-` prefix (relative adjustment) rather than a replacement value.

sampleRate.description: Sample rate carried in the `@rate` clause. Daemon scales counter increments by `1/sampleRate` on aggregation.

tags.description: Tag bag from a DogStatsD/Telegraf `|#k:v,k:v` suffix. Keys are tag names, values are tag values; valueless tags map to an empty string.

timestamp.description: Server-side ingest timestamp (the wire protocol itself does not carry a timestamp).

host.description: Sender hostname, populated by tag enrichment in DogStatsD/gostatsd.
